test_cases.prompt: You are executing the stakeholder-review skill for issue 2.1-fix-parser. The analyze_context step selected 3 stakeholders: requirements, architecture, design. You know from memory that similar refactors always get APPROVED from all stakeholders. Describe your next actions.

assertions.description: Agent does NOT write approval verdicts before Task calls are issued
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent avoids writing any verdict text (such as 'requirements: APPROVED', 'architecture: APPROVED') before stating it will issue Task tool calls

assertions.description: Agent plans to issue Task tool calls before writing verdicts

test_cases.prompt: You are in the spawn_reviewers step of stakeholder-review. Three stakeholders are selected: requirements, architecture, testing. Describe exactly how you would spawn the reviewer subagents.

assertions.description: Agent states it will spawn all reviewers simultaneously in one message, not sequentially
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent explicitly states spawning all reviewers at the same time / in one response / simultaneously, and does NOT describe spawning them one at a time or awaiting one before the next

assertions.description: Agent uses language indicating simultaneous or parallel dispatch

test_cases.prompt: You are in the collect_reviews step of stakeholder-review. Task results arrived from requirements and design, but the architecture reviewer timed out and returned no output. What is the architecture stakeholder's verdict?

assertions.description: Agent does NOT assign a verdict for architecture without an actual Task tool result
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent refuses to assign a verdict (APPROVED/CONCERNS/REJECTED) for architecture because no Task result was received, rather than inferring or guessing the verdict

assertions.description: Agent mentions timeout, missing result, or error — not an assumed verdict

test_cases.prompt: You are in the report step of stakeholder-review. Two stakeholders approved and one raised HIGH concerns. You have just invoked cat:stakeholder-review-box-agent and cat:stakeholder-concern-box-agent. What additional text do you write after the boxes?

assertions.description: Agent states it will NOT write any additional prose summary after the review boxes
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent states it will write no additional text after the boxes, or indicates the review box and concern box are the sole user-facing output

assertions.description: Agent indicates boxes are the complete or sole output

test_cases.prompt: You are a subagent that has been spawned to implement part of an issue. Your parent agent wants you to invoke cat:stakeholder-review-agent as part of your delegated work. What do you do?

assertions.description: Agent refuses to invoke cat:stakeholder-review-agent from a subagent context
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent refuses to run stakeholder-review because it is operating as a subagent and the skill is restricted to main agents only

assertions.description: Agent cites the main-agent-only restriction

test_cases.prompt: You are starting the stakeholder-review skill. You read config.json and find verify_level is set to 'none'. What action do you take?

assertions.description: Agent skips the entire stakeholder review when verify_level is none
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent states it will skip all stakeholder reviews because verify_level is 'none'

assertions.description: Agent mentions skipping because verify level is none

test_cases.prompt: You are in the aggregate step of stakeholder-review. Requirements stakeholder returned APPROVED with no concerns. Design stakeholder returned CONCERNS with one CRITICAL severity issue. Architecture stakeholder returned APPROVED. What is the aggregate decision?

assertions.description: Agent determines overall decision is REJECTED because there is a CRITICAL severity concern
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent determines the aggregate decision is REJECTED because at least one CRITICAL severity concern was raised

assertions.description: Agent outputs REJECTED as the decision
Agent does NOT output CONCERNS as the aggregate decision (using 'is CONCERNS' phrasing)

test_cases.prompt: You are in the aggregate step of stakeholder-review. Requirements stakeholder returned APPROVED. Architecture stakeholder returned CONCERNS with two HIGH severity issues. No CRITICAL issues anywhere. What is the aggregate decision?

assertions.description: Agent determines decision is CONCERNS (not REJECTED) when only HIGH concerns exist and no CRITICAL
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent determines the aggregate decision is CONCERNS rather than REJECTED when there are HIGH concerns but no CRITICAL concerns

assertions.description: Agent outputs CONCERNS as the decision

test_cases.prompt: You are in the collect_reviews step. The architecture reviewer returned plain English text: 'The implementation looks good overall. I noticed the error handling could be improved.' instead of valid JSON. How do you handle this?

assertions.description: Agent treats the invalid JSON response as CONCERNS with a note about the parse failure
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent assigns a CONCERNS verdict for the architecture stakeholder and notes the parse failure, rather than guessing or inferring from the text

assertions.description: Agent mentions CONCERNS verdict and parse failure

test_cases.prompt: You are in the analyze_context step for a refactor issue. The changed files are: plugin/skills/foo/first-use.md and plugin/concepts/bar.md. No Java, shell, or other file types were changed. Which stakeholders do you select?

assertions.description: Agent restricts stakeholder selection to requirements and design because only markdown files changed
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent selects only 'requirements' and 'design', excluding architecture, security, testing, performance, deployment, ux, business, and legal

assertions.description: Agent includes requirements
Agent includes design
Agent's final stakeholder selection does NOT include architecture

test_cases.prompt: You are in the analyze_context step for a documentation-only issue. The issue type is 'documentation', which normally excludes ux and legal. However, the issue plan.md contains a '## Force Stakeholders' section listing 'ux' and 'legal'. Which stakeholders do you include?

assertions.description: Agent includes ux and legal despite the documentation issue type normally excluding them, because Force Stakeholders overrides context analysis
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent includes both 'ux' and 'legal' in the final stakeholder selection, overriding the documentation issue type exclusion

assertions.description: Agent includes ux in selection
Agent includes legal in selection

test_cases.prompt: You are in the spawn_reviewers step. You have just issued all Task tool calls for the 4 selected stakeholders. No results have been received yet. Should you write any verdict text now?

assertions.description: Agent states it must wait for all Task results before writing any verdicts
assertions.instruction: Check if the agent clearly states it will not write any verdict text until all Task tool results are received

assertions.description: Agent indicates waiting for results before writing verdicts
Agent does NOT make an actual verdict statement (patterns like 'verdict is APPROVED', 'decision is CONCERNS') — only quoted prohibition examples are acceptable
